architecture.summary: SLiNT injects knowledge graph structural context into a frozen LLM backbone via three modular components: (1) Structure-Guided Neighborhood Enhancement (SGNE) retrieves pseudo-neighbors as text to enrich sparse entity representations; (2) Dynamic Hard Contrastive Learning (DHCL) applies entity-level contrastive supervision with interpolated hard positives/negatives; (3) Gradient-Decoupled Dual Injection (GDDI) performs token-level structural intervention via LoRA adapters while keeping core LLM parameters frozen. Information flows from KG structure → text-enriched prompts → LLM → contrastive loss supervision → adapted representations for link prediction.

biblio.title: SLiNT: Structure-aware Language Model with Injection and Contrastive Training for Knowledge Graph Completion

claims.statement: SLiNT achieves superior or competitive performance compared with both embedding-based and generation-based baselines on knowledge graph link prediction
Structure-Guided Neighborhood Enhancement (SGNE) retrieves pseudo-neighbors to enrich sparse entities and mitigate missing context
Dynamic Hard Contrastive Learning (DHCL) introduces fine-grained supervision by interpolating hard positives and negatives to resolve entity-level ambiguity
Gradient-Decoupled Dual Injection (GDDI) performs token-level structure-aware intervention while preserving the core LLM parameters
LLMs' limited exploitation of structural signals often results in structural sparsity and semantic ambiguity, especially under incomplete or zero-shot settings
